scores.materiality_reasoning: Virtue is a purely abstract moral/philosophical trait.
scores.generality_reasoning: Virtue represents a foundational ethical axis in Greek thought.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Character excellence is viewed as a lifelong structural trait.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is a biologically fixed group in the author's context.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author reports on philosophical schools active or influential in his era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a pure philosophical assertion of what virtue 'is'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Capacity for virtue is not part of the definition of gender.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to reside in the city vs exile is a matter of physical freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Exile/residency is a foundational axis of legal personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Banishment is typically a permanent or life-altering legal change.

scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites historical accounts and legal outcomes as recorded facts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of banker does not entail being banished.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Imprisonment and exile involve physical detention and loss of property.

scores.persistence_reasoning: Imprisonment until death and fleeing the country are lifelong outcomes.

scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses historical events from the 4th century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific historical legal consequences as plain facts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Expatriation is not part of the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Authority to command and train free persons has direct social effect.

scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported fact of a social arrangement with authorial framing.
